entities.name: Duty of Care Services
entities.description: The comprehensive program of services defined in Section 3, encompassing itinerary visibility, risk monitoring, pre-trip preparation, in-trip monitoring, incident response, and post-incident reporting.

source_anchor.source_text: SCOPE OF DUTY OF CARE SERVICES

entities.name: Itinerary Visibility and Traveler Locate
entities.description: A foundational Duty of Care service providing Client with real-time visibility of active travelers through reporting dashboards (showing status by destination, travel dates, and booking status) and an optional interactive geospatial map displaying locations with risk ratings and threat alerts.

source_anchor.source_text: ### 3.1 Itinerary Visibility and Traveler Locate

entities.authorization_details: Interactive global map component is included where specified in the SOW

entities.name: Reporting Dashboard
entities.description: A technology platform providing Client access to real-time visibility of active travelers, filterable and sortable by destination, travel dates, and booking status.

source_anchor.source_text: reporting dashboards showing active Travelers by destination, travel dates, and booking status

entities.name: Interactive Global Map
entities.description: A geospatial visualization platform displaying traveler locations based on itinerary data, overlaid with destination-level risk ratings and active threat alerts, available conditionally based on SOW agreement.

source_anchor.source_text: interactive global map displaying Traveler locations (based on itinerary data) overlaid with destination risk ratings and active threat alerts

relationships.description: Direct Travel provides the comprehensive Duty of Care Services program, encompassing itinerary visibility, risk monitoring, and incident response capabilities.
Direct Travel provides the Itinerary Visibility and Traveler Locate service, including capture, maintenance, and reporting of traveler itinerary data.
Travel booked through Approved Channels enables Direct Travel to capture itinerary data required to deliver the Itinerary Visibility and Traveler Locate service.
Use of Approved Channels for all travel bookings enables the full Duty of Care Services program to function effectively.
The Itinerary Visibility service is delivered through the Reporting Dashboard platform, which provides Client with real-time visibility of active travelers by destination, date, and booking status.

The Itinerary Visibility service includes an optional interactive global map platform that displays traveler locations with destination risk ratings and threat alerts, available where included in the SOW.
Direct Travel operates and maintains the Reporting Dashboard platform providing Client access to traveler visibility information.
Direct Travel operates and maintains the Interactive Global Map platform displaying traveler locations and destination risk intelligence.
Client is a party to the Statement of Work which specifies which services and platforms (including optional Interactive Global Map access) are included in the Duty of Care engagement.
Direct Travel is a party to the Statement of Work which defines Client-specific service configurations and optional service modules.
When an Incident occurs, Direct Travel uses available itinerary data to identify which Travelers are impacted and require assistance or information.

entities.name: Traveler
entities.description: A Client employee or other authorized person traveling on Client business, referenced throughout Section 5.1 in the context of incident impact assessments across severity levels.

source_anchor.source_text: limited direct impact on Travelers; ... may impact Travelers; ... likely impacting one or more Travelers

entities.name: Level 1
entities.description: Advisory classification for minor disruption with limited direct impact on Travelers; informational alert only. Examples: Localized weather warning, minor transportation delay, low-level protest in non-traveler area.

source_anchor.source_text: Level 1 | Advisory | Minor disruption with limited direct impact on Travelers; informational alert only | Localized weather warning, minor transportation delay, low-level protest in non-traveler area
source_anchor.source_section: SEC-05, Section 5.1 Severity Level Definitions table

entities.name: Level 2
entities.description: Monitor classification for moderate disruption that may impact Travelers; targeted alerts issued; optional welfare check. Examples: Significant weather event approaching, regional transportation strike announced, elevated security alert in destination city.

source_anchor.source_text: Level 2 | Monitor | Moderate disruption that may impact Travelers; targeted alerts issued; optional welfare check | Significant weather event approaching, regional transportation strike announced, elevated security alert in destination city
source_anchor.source_section: SEC-05, Section 5.1 Severity Level Definitions table

entities.name: Level 3
entities.description: Action Required classification for significant disruption likely impacting one or more Travelers; proactive outreach required. Section text incomplete; visible example: Major weather event.

source_anchor.source_text: Level 3 | Action Required | Significant disruption likely impacting one or more Travelers; proactive outreach required | Major weather event (hurr

entities.name: Incident
entities.description: A discrete event that may reasonably impact traveler safety, security, health, or ability to continue travel; referenced throughout Section 6.1 as the trigger for Client escalation roster activation.

source_anchor.source_text: First point of contact for all Incidents

entities.description: The Level 1 through Level 4 tiered classification system for incidents; Section 6.1 references Levels 3–4 as the trigger for escalation to Corporate Security.

source_anchor.source_text: Contact for security-related Incidents (Level 3–4)

relationships.description: Client is assigned the obligation to provide Direct Travel with a 24/7 escalation roster containing specified contact roles with required information.
Primary Travel Program Owner role is designated by Client as the first point of contact for all incidents on the escalation roster.
Alternate Travel Program Contact role is designated by Client to serve as backup when the Primary Travel Program Owner is unavailable.
After-Hours Duty Contact role is designated by Client to handle incidents occurring outside normal Client business hours.
Corporate Security contact role is designated by Client to handle security-related incidents classified as Level 3 or Level 4 severity.
Human Resources Duty Contact role is designated by Client to handle incidents with human resources, employee welfare, or workforce implications.
Incidents classified as Level 3 or Level 4 severity in security context escalate to the Corporate Security contact on the escalation roster.
